perturbation.original_text: The parties agree as follows with respect to treatment of the Confidential Information: 1. Independent Contractor shall use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of performing the Services specified in the applicable SOW and not for any other purpose. Except to the extent permitted by Section 3 below, Independent Contractor will not disclose the Confidential Information, in whole or in part, to any other party. In fulfilling its obligations under this Agreement, Independent Contractor shall use at least the same standard of care it uses to protect its own information of similar kind, but not less than a reasonable standard of care.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree as follows with respect to treatment of the Confidential Information: 1. Independent Contractor may use the Confidential Information for the purpose of performing the Services specified in the applicable SOW, but is also allowed to use it for internal training purposes. Except to the extent permitted by Section 3 below, Independent Contractor will not disclose the Confidential Information, in whole or in part, to any other party. In fulfilling its obligations under this Agreement, Independent Contractor shall use at least the same standard of care it uses to protect its own information of similar kind, but not less than a reasonable standard of care.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'shall' to 'may' introduces ambiguity, weakening the obligation of the Independent Contractor to only use the Confidential Information for the SOW. By also allowing internal training purposes, it contradicts the initial strict restriction. This creates an in-text contradiction with later sections that assume strict confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed by the Company to Independent Contractor shall be and shall remain the Company's property. Upon termination of the Agreement, Independent Contractor shall redeliver all tangible Confidential Information furnished by the Company. Except to the extent Independent Contractor is advised in writing by counsel that such action is prohibited by law, Independent Contractor will also destroy all written material, memoranda, notes, and other writings or recordings whatsoever prepared by it based upon, containing, or otherwise reflecting any Confidential Information. Any Confidential Information that is not returned or destroyed, including without limitation any oral Confidential Information, shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this Exhibit B.
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly limits the use of Confidential Information to performing Services specified in the SOW. The changed text weakens this restriction by allowing the use of Confidential Information for internal training purposes, in addition to the SOW. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which mandates the return or destruction of all Confidential Information upon termination of the Agreement and asserts that any remaining Confidential Information, including oral information, is subject to strict confidentiality obligations. Allowing internal training introduces a use case not covered by the SOW and increases the risk of information leaks or misuse, making it harder to ensure the confidentiality of the Company's property and adhere to the obligation to return or destroy all information, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2. The term "Confidential Information" shall be deemed not to include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than (a) as a result of a disclosure by Independent Contractor or any other person who directly or indirectly receives such information from the Independent Contractor or (b) in violation of a confidentiality obligation to the Company known to Independent Contractor or (ii) is or becomes available to Independent Contractor on a non-confidential basis from a source which is entitled to disclose it to Independent Contractor or (iii) is independently developed by Independent Contractor without benefit of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. The term "Confidential Information" shall be deemed not to include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than (a) as a result of a disclosure by Independent Contractor or any other person who directly or indirectly receives such information from the Independent Contractor or (b) in violation of a confidentiality obligation to the Company known to Independent Contractor or (ii) is or becomes available to Independent Contractor on a non-confidential basis from a source which is entitled to disclose it to Independent Contractor, or (iv) becomes known to Independent Contractor through casual conversation.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of '(iv) becomes known to Independent Contractor through casual conversation' broadens the exceptions to 'Confidential Information,' creating potential conflicts. The original definition strictly protects against disclosures but this new clause makes the term 'confidential Information' weaker. This directly contradicts the requirements in paragraph 1 of using information solely for the SOW.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties agree as follows with respect to treatment of the Confidential Information: 1. Independent Contractor shall use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of performing the Services specified in the applicable SOW and not for any other purpose. Except to the extent permitted by Section 3 below, Independent Contractor will not disclose the Confidential Information, in whole or in part, to any other party. In fulfilling its obligations under this Agreement, Independent Contractor shall use at least the same standard of care it uses to protect its own information of similar kind, but not less than a reasonable standard of care.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds '(iv) becomes known to Independent Contractor through casual conversation' to the exceptions of 'Confidential Information'. The original text in paragraph 1 states that the Independent Contractor shall use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of performing the Services specified in the applicable SOW and not for any other purpose and will not disclose the Confidential Information. The added clause now permits the Independent Contractor to use and disclose information obtained through casual conversation, which contradicts the original intent of keeping Confidential Information protected and using it solely for the SOW. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as it weakens the confidentiality obligations. This aligns with the definition provided that ambiguities occur when key terms or obligations are defined inconsistently, making enforcement difficult or interpretation unclear.

perturbation.original_text: 3. In the event that Independent Contractor is required by law or by interrogatories, requests for information or documents, subpoena, Civil Investigative Demand, or similar process to disclose any information supplied to Independent Contractor pursuant to the Agreement, including without limitation the Confidential Information or any other information the disclosure of which is restricted by the terms of this Exhibit B, Independent Contractor will provide the Company with prompt prior written notice of such request or requirement so that the Company may seek an appropriate protective order.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. In the event that Independent Contractor is required by law or by interrogatories, requests for information or documents, subpoena, Civil Investigative Demand, or similar process to disclose any information supplied to Independent Contractor pursuant to the Agreement, including without limitation the Confidential Information or any other information the disclosure of which is restricted by the terms of this Exhibit B, Independent Contractor will make reasonable effort to provide the Company with prompt prior written notice of such request or requirement so that the Company may seek an appropriate protective order.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will provide' to 'will make reasonable effort to provide' weakens the obligation to notify the company of required disclosures. This creates conflict with paragraph 5, because if the company is not notified, they can't seek injunctive relief.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Independent Contractor acknowledges and agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Exhibit B by Independent Contractor and that the Company shall be entitled to specific performance, including without limitation injunctive relief, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for breach of this Exhibit B but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity. Independent Contractor agrees to reimburse the Company for costs and expenses (including without limitation attorneys' fees) incurred by the Company in connection with the enforcement of this Exhibit B.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the Independent Contractor 'will provide' notice, creating a firm obligation. The changed text weakens this to 'will make reasonable effort to provide'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which grants the Company injunctive relief for any breach of Exhibit B. If the Independent Contractor only makes a 'reasonable effort' but fails to notify the Company, the Company cannot seek injunctive relief. This creates a direct conflict because the original obligation was absolute and now is conditional, preventing the company from obtaining a remedy for the breach.

perturbation.original_text: 4. All Confidential Information disclosed by the Company to Independent Contractor shall be and shall remain the Company's property. Upon termination of the Agreement, Independent Contractor shall redeliver all tangible Confidential Information furnished by the Company. Except to the extent Independent Contractor is advised in writing by counsel that such action is prohibited by law, Independent Contractor will also destroy all written material, memoranda, notes, and other writings or recordings whatsoever prepared by it based upon, containing, or otherwise reflecting any Confidential Information. Any Confidential Information that is not returned or destroyed, including without limitation any oral Confidential Information, shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this Exhibit B.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. All Confidential Information disclosed by the Company to Independent Contractor shall be and shall remain the Company's property. Upon termination of the Agreement, Independent Contractor shall redeliver all tangible Confidential Information furnished by the Company, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by both parties. Except to the extent Independent Contractor is advised in writing by counsel that such action is prohibited by law, Independent Contractor will also destroy all written material, memoranda, notes, and other writings or recordings whatsoever prepared by it based upon, containing, or otherwise reflecting any Confidential Information. Any Confidential Information that is not returned or destroyed, including without limitation any oral Confidential Information, shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this Exhibit B.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'unless otherwise agreed to in writing by both parties' to the redelivery obligation introduces flexibility that contradicts the initial statement that the Confidential Information shall remain the Company's property. This directly impacts paragraph 1, which states the independent contractor will not disclose the Confidential information, but this modification allows the possibility of holding onto that information after termination. It contradicts the purpose of the confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties agree as follows with respect to treatment of the Confidential Information: 1. Independent Contractor shall use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of performing the Services specified in the applicable SOW and not for any other purpose. Except to the extent permitted by Section 3 below, Independent Contractor will not disclose the Confidential Information, in whole or in part, to any other party. In fulfilling its obligations under this Agreement, Independent Contractor shall use at least the same standard of care it uses to protect its own information of similar kind, but not less than a reasonable standard of care.
perturbation.justification: The addition of 'unless otherwise agreed to in writing by both parties' in the Changed Text directly contradicts paragraph 1 of the Contradicted Text, which states that the Independent Contractor 'will not disclose the Confidential Information'. The modification allows for the possibility of the Independent Contractor retaining and potentially disclosing confidential information after termination if agreed upon in writing, directly conflicting with the original confidentiality obligation. This introduces an ambiguity regarding the contractor's obligations with Confidential Information, which creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Independent Contractor acknowledges and agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Exhibit B by Independent Contractor and that the Company shall be entitled to specific performance, including without limitation injunctive relief, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for breach of this Exhibit B but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity. Independent Contractor agrees to reimburse the Company for costs and expenses (including without limitation attorneys' fees) incurred by the Company in connection with the enforcement of this Exhibit B.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Independent Contractor acknowledges that money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Exhibit B by Independent Contractor and that the Company may be entitled to specific performance, including without limitation injunctive relief, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for breach of this Exhibit B but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity. Independent Contractor agrees to reimburse the Company for reasonable costs and expenses (including without limitation attorneys' fees) incurred by the Company in connection with the enforcement of this Exhibit B.
perturbation.explanation: The changes from 'would not be' to 'may not be' and 'shall be' to 'may be' weakens the assurance of specific performance and injunctive relief. By changing 'costs and expenses' to 'reasonable costs and expenses', it creates ambiguity on what is considered reasonable. This contradicts the purpose of paragraph 1, on Independent Contractor using the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of the SOW, because if a breach happens, it makes the remedy unclear and uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties agree as follows with respect to treatment of the Confidential Information: 1. Independent Contractor shall use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of performing the Services specified in the applicable SOW and not for any other purpose. Except to the extent permitted by Section 3 below, Independent Contractor will not disclose the Confidential Information, in whole or in part, to any other party. In fulfilling its obligations under this Agreement, Independent Contractor shall use at least the same standard of care it uses to protect its own information of similar kind, but not less than a reasonable standard of care.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'would not be' to 'may not be' and 'shall be' to 'may be' weakens the assurance of specific performance and injunctive relief. By changing 'costs and expenses' to 'reasonable costs and expenses', it creates ambiguity on what is considered reasonable. This introduces ambiguity and weakens the enforceability of remedies, which contradicts the contradicted section's aim to ensure the Independent Contractor uses Confidential Information solely for the SOW purpose. The original text ensures the company shall be entitled to specific performance, but the changed text makes it uncertain.

perturbation.original_text: 6. If any provision of this Exhibit B is not enforceable in whole or in part, the remaining provisions of this Exhibit B shall not be affected thereby. No failure or delay in exercising any right, power, or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power, or privilege hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. If any provision of this Exhibit B is not enforceable in whole or in part, the remaining provisions of this Exhibit B shall be interpreted in light of the unenforceable provision. No failure or delay in exercising any right, power, or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power, or privilege hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall not be affected thereby' to 'shall be interpreted in light of the unenforceable provision' introduces uncertainty. It suggests that an unenforceable clause could still influence the interpretation of other clauses, creating conflicts. The 'unenforceable provision' can create a bias of interpretation in the contract and weakens the obligation of the contractor. This creates contradiction with paragraph 4, because it could be used to argue the clause in section 4 is unenforceable or interpreted weakly and potentially not return or destroy confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed by the Company to Independent Contractor shall be and shall remain the Company's property. Upon termination of the Agreement, Independent Contractor shall redeliver all tangible Confidential Information furnished by the Company. Except to the extent Independent Contractor is advised in writing by counsel that such action is prohibited by law, Independent Contractor will also destroy all written material, memoranda, notes, and other writings or recordings whatsoever prepared by it based upon, containing, or otherwise reflecting any Confidential Information. Any Confidential Information that is not returned or destroyed, including without limitation any oral Confidential Information, shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this Exhibit B.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies clause 6 of Exhibit B, stating that if a provision is unenforceable, the remaining provisions should be interpreted in light of the unenforceable one. This introduces ambiguity and potential bias in interpreting the contract, creating uncertainty about the enforceability of other clauses. This directly contradicts the clause regarding confidential information, which requires the independent contractor to return or destroy confidential information. The change allows for an argument that the confidential information clause could be weakly interpreted, leading to a potential failure to return/destroy confidential information, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.
